page: Google Cloud Platform g2-standard-4 is a graphics-optimized virtual machine featuring an x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with an Intel Xeon CPU providing 4 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical cores with hyper-threading, operating at a base clock speed of 2.2 GHz. The system includes 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB per core. Acceleration is driven by a single bundled NVIDIA L4 GPU based on the Ada Lovelace architecture, which features 22 GB of dedicated VRAM. Local storage is not included in this configuration, and it offers zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data reveals a distinct contrast between CPU-bound tasks and GPU-accelerated workloads. Standard CPU performance metrics, such as stress-ng and Geekbench single-core and multi-core tests, fall into the bottom 25% tier. Similarly, memory bandwidth and file compression speeds are weak, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is rated in the bottom 10%. However, the instance excels in machine learning tasks. Large language model (LLM) inference benchmarks place this server in the top 10% tier, achieving 2296.42 tokens per second for prompt processing and 52.18 tokens per second for text generation on a medium 7B model.

The qualitative cost efficiency of the g2-standard-4 is highly dependent on the utilization of its GPU resources. For general-purpose compute or database operations, the weak CPU and memory performance represent a poor resource-to-cost tradeoff. Conversely, for workloads that can offload computation to the NVIDIA L4 GPU, such as small to medium LLM inference, graphics rendering, and AI model deployment, the instance provides high resource density. This server is best fit for low-latency AI inference, media processing, and GPU-accelerated development environments rather than CPU-heavy web serving or database management.
description: Google Cloud Platform g2-standard-4 is a graphics-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16.0 GB of system memory, and a bundled NVIDIA L4 GPU with 22 GB of VRAM. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this server does not include local storage. Performance benchmarks show a clear tradeoff: standard CPU and memory operations, including stress-ng and Geekbench tests, rank in the bottom 25%, whereas GPU-driven workloads perform exceptionally well. Specifically, LLM inference speeds for small and medium models reach the top 10% tier, processing 2296.42 tokens per second on a 7B model. Consequently, the instance is highly cost-efficient for specialized GPU tasks like AI inference and graphics rendering, but represents a poor tradeoff for CPU-bound applications or database hosting.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform g2-standard-4 features 4 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA L4 GPU with 22 GB VRAM. It delivers top-tier LLM inference performance despite weak baseline CPU benchmarks.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform g2-standard-4 features 4 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA L4 GPU, delivering top-tier performance for LLM inference workloads.
tagline: A GPU-accelerated virtual machine delivering top-tier low-latency LLM inference performance paired with dedicated hardware acceleration.
bullet_points: Equipped with 1 NVIDIA L4 GPU featuring 22 GB of VRAM
Features 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 16.0 GB of system memory
Top-tier performance in LLM inference for small and medium models
Weak baseline CPU and memory performance benchmarks
No local storage included in the default configuration
Best suited for AI inference, graphics rendering, and GPU-accelerated workloads
